taxonomy: Dortmanna boivinii is a species of flowering plant in the family Haloragaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and is also known as the dwarf water-dilla. The classification of Dortmanna boivinii is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, phylum - Angiospermophyta, class - Magnoliopsida, order - Haloragales, family - Haloragaceae, genus - Dortmanna, species - D. boivinii. Dortmanna boivinii is a relatively small plant that thrives in aquatic environments. It is an important component of its ecosystem, providing food and shelter for various aquatic species.
uses: Dortmanna boivinii is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.
